prs.title: feat: add barAlign prop for left/right/center bar alignment
prs.body:
- Add barAlign prop to BarChart (left, center, right)
- Modify bar positioning logic to support alignment
- Default to center for backward compatibility

Fixes #6296

prs.title: Fix: Ken drops databases on nodedown
prs.body:
## Description
This PR fixes an issue where Ken (the background indexer) would drop a database from its indexing queue if `fabric:design_docs/1` returned a `nodedown` or `maintenance_mode` error.

Previously, these errors were caught and treated as an empty list of design documents (`{ok, []}`), causing Ken to believe there was nothing to index and removing the database from the pending queue.

## Changes
- Modified `ken_server:design_docs/1` to propagate `{error, {nodedown, ...}}` and `{error, {maintenance_mode, ...}}` instead of swallowing them.
- Modified `ken_server:update_db_indexes/2` to handle these specific errors by exiting with `resubmit`. This triggers Ken's built-in retry mechanism, ensuring the database is added back to the queue to be retried later.

## Testing
- Added a reproduction test case in `src/ken/test/ken_repro_test.erl` (if applicable to include in PR).
- Verified that `nodedown` errors now trigger a resubmit instead of a silent drop.

Fixes #5392<!-- Thank you for your contribution!

     Please file this form by replacing the Markdown comments
     with your text. If a section needs no action - remove it.

     Also remember, that CouchDB uses the Review-Then-Commit (RTC) model
     of code collaboration. Positive feedback is represented +1 from committers
     and negative is a -1. The -1 also means veto, and needs to be addressed
     to proceed. Once there are no objections, the PR can be merged by a
     CouchDB committer.

     See: http://couchdb.apache.org/bylaws.html#decisions for more info. -->

prs.title: docs: Added Quick Start section in README
prs.body:
## Did you read the Contributor Guide?

Yes, I have read the [Contributor Rules](https://sedona.apache.org/latest/community/rule/) and [Contributor Development Guide](https://sedona.apache.org/latest/community/develop/)

## Is this PR related to a ticket?

Yes, this PR is related to Issue #2187 and follows the [DOCS] naming format.
(It contributes to improving documentation but does not fully close the issue.)

## What changes were proposed in this PR?

Added a Quick Start section for Python, Scala/Java, and R, each linking to the detailed setup guides on the Sedona website.
This makes it easier for new users to get started with their preferred language quickly.

## How was this patch tested?

- Ran pre-commit run --all-files — all checks passed.
- Verified links and Markdown formatting on GitHub preview.

## Did this PR include necessary documentation updates?

- Yes, documentation improved for onboarding.
- No code or API changes.

prs.title: docs: add Quick Start section to README
prs.body:
This PR enhances the README.md by adding a new Quick Start section to help new users get started with Apache Sedona more easily.
It addresses issue #2187.

The update provides:
Step-by-step installation guidance for Python, Scala/Java, and R.
Minimal “Hello World” code examples for each language.
Direct links to documentation and setup guides.

Goal: Improve onboarding and accessibility for first-time users and contributors by offering a concise, language-specific entry point.

issues.title: Descriptive README.md
issues.body: make a well structured and well explaining README.md about our project!

issues.title: Clean and Systematic File Structure
issues.body:
## The project should be divided into two main directories:

### api/ — for all backend logic

### client/ — for all frontend (React) code


```
project-root/
│
├── api/                         # Backend (Node.js / Express)
│   ├── Prisma/                  # Prisma models (schemas)
│   ├── controllers/             # Backend logic for routes
│   ├── routes/                  # API route definitions
│   ├── middlewares/             # Authentication, error handling, etc.
│   ├── utils/                   # Helper functions (e.g., error handling, tokens)
│   ├── server.js                # Main Express server entry point
│   └── package.json
│
├── client/                      # Frontend (React)
│   ├── public/                  # Static assets (index.html, icons, etc.)
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── components/          # Reusable UI components
│   │   ├── pages/               # Page-level components (e.g., Login, Messages)
│   │   ├── utils/               # Helper files (API calls, constants, hooks)
│   │   ├── App.js               # Root component
│   │   └── index.js             # React DOM entry point
│   └── package.json
│
├── .env                         # Environment variables
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
```
